item.title: The Attack Surface in Your Pocketâand How Scattered Spider Socially Engineers Their Way Inside
item.description: Close the mobile security gap in financial services with actionable steps to protect personal devices from threats like Scattered Spider.

item.title: With iOS 26.2 Release, Apple Reverses Course on Shutdown.log
item.description: iOS 26's shutdown.log changes erased traces of Pegasus and Predator spyware, but Apple reverted the fix in 26.2.

item.title: Breaking Down ZeroDayRAT - New Spyware Targeting Android and iOS
item.description: ZeroDayRAT is a commercial mobile spyware platform sold via Telegram that gives attackers full remote access to Android and iOS devices

item.title: How Attackers Run Smishing Campaigns (And Why They Work)
item.description: Smishing is surging. We break down how cybercriminals source phone numbers, launch spoofed SMS campaigns, and trick victims into handing over credentials

item.title: iVerify Extends Cloudflare Integrations With WARP
item.description: We're bringing Cloudflare Zero Trust (WARP) to mobile devices, delivering real-time posture signals directly into Cloudflare access policies.

item.title: Mobile Is the New Initial Access Vector for Financial Institutions
item.description: As banks harden endpoints, attackers are shifting to mobile. Real mobile spyware, zero-click attacks, why MDM fails, and why BYOD phones are prime targets.

item.title: Meet Cellik - A New Android RAT With Play Store Integration
item.description: Discover how Cellik Android RAT enables full device surveillance with live screen access, keylogging, app injection, and Play Store APK wrapping.

item.title: Intellexaâs Predator Exploit Chain: New Details Emerge After Google Publishes Samples
item.description: Googleâs new research reveals how Intellexaâs Predator spyware exploits zero-days, abuses iOS trust mechanisms, and continues evolving despite sanctions
